policies.title: Returns, refunds, and exchanges for merchandise

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Defines how PBT handles returns, refunds, exchanges, and faulty merchandise worldwide, subject to regional consumer laws.

sections.heading: When it applies
sections.content: All PBT Store merchandise purchases; regional overlays take precedence where they apply.

sections.heading: Rules
sections.content:
- Change of mind: within 30 days; unused; original condition; tags and packaging intact.
- Faulty items: 12-month warranty for manufacturing defects; major fault means refund or replacement; minor fault means repair or replacement.
- Proof of purchase required (order email, receipt, or bank record).
- Customer must contact support before returning; photos for faults when possible.
- Return shipping: change-of-mind customer pays; faulty items PBT pays return and replacement shipping.
- Refund timing: refunds to original method within 5 business days after receiving the return; store credit may be faster.

sections.heading: Allowed actions
sections.content:
- Provide refunds, exchanges, or store credit within policy limits.
- Request photos or other evidence of a fault.
- Offer goodwill solutions within approval limits.

sections.heading: Disallowed actions
sections.content:
- Refuse legitimate warranty claims.
- Issue refunds without proof of purchase.
- Override consumer law rights.

sections.heading: Required wording
sections.content: "We're happy to help with a return or refund in line with our policy and your local consumer rights."

sections.heading: Escalation
sections.content: Legal disputes, unclear fault classification, or high-value orders.

sections.heading: Examples
sections.content:
- A customer changes their mind within 30 days and the item is unused with tags intact; the return is eligible.
- A manufacturing defect appears after 6 months; the warranty applies and a remedy is required.

# Summary
Defines how PBT handles returns, refunds, exchanges, and faulty merchandise worldwide, subject to regional consumer laws.

# When it applies
All PBT Store merchandise purchases; regional overlays take precedence where they apply.

# Rules
- Change of mind: within 30 days; unused; original condition; tags and packaging intact.
- Faulty items: 12-month warranty for manufacturing defects; major fault means refund or replacement; minor fault means repair or replacement.
- Proof of purchase required (order email, receipt, or bank record).
- Customer must contact support before returning; photos for faults when possible.
- Return shipping: change-of-mind customer pays; faulty items PBT pays return and replacement shipping.
- Refund timing: refunds to original method within 5 business days after receiving the return; store credit may be faster.

# Allowed actions
- Provide refunds, exchanges, or store credit within policy limits.
- Request photos or other evidence of a fault.
- Offer goodwill solutions within approval limits.

# Disallowed actions
- Refuse legitimate warranty claims.
- Issue refunds without proof of purchase.
- Override consumer law rights.

# Required wording
"We're happy to help with a return or refund in line with our policy and your local consumer rights."

# Escalation
Legal disputes, unclear fault classification, or high-value orders.

# Examples
- A customer changes their mind within 30 days and the item is unused with tags intact; the return is eligible.
- A manufacturing defect appears after 6 months; the warranty applies and a remedy is required.

policies.title: Shipping and delivery for merchandise

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Shipping coverage, delivery estimates, carriers, duties, and tracking for merchandise orders.

sections.heading: When it applies
sections.content: All merchandise orders.

sections.heading: Rules
sections.content:
- Rates are calculated at checkout; free shipping may apply if a threshold is met.
- Delivery times are estimates only; they are not guaranteed.
- Carriers vary by region (AusPost, DHL, UPS, FedEx, USPS, or local equivalents).
- Tracking is provided on dispatch; delays do not always mean a parcel is lost.
- Duties and taxes are the customer's responsibility unless DDP is selected at checkout.
- Lost or delayed: if the carrier confirms loss, offer a replacement or refund; delays alone do not qualify for an automatic refund.

sections.heading: Allowed actions
sections.content:
- Help customers track shipments and interpret tracking updates.
- Open carrier investigations when deliveries appear delayed or missing.

sections.heading: Disallowed actions
sections.content:
- Guarantee delivery dates or transit times.
- Cover duties or taxes unless explicitly authorized.

sections.heading: Required wording
sections.content: "Delivery times are estimates provided by carriers and may vary depending on location and customs processing."

sections.heading: Escalation
sections.content: Confirmed loss cases or systemic carrier delays.

sections.heading: Examples
sections.content:
- A parcel is held by customs; advise the customer about estimated delays and duties.
- The carrier confirms a shipment is lost; offer a replacement or refund.

---

# Summary
Shipping coverage, delivery estimates, carriers, duties, and tracking for merchandise orders.

# When it applies
All merchandise orders.

# Rules
- Rates are calculated at checkout; free shipping may apply if a threshold is met.
- Delivery times are estimates only; they are not guaranteed.
- Carriers vary by region (AusPost, DHL, UPS, FedEx, USPS, or local equivalents).
- Tracking is provided on dispatch; delays do not always mean a parcel is lost.
- Duties and taxes are the customer's responsibility unless DDP is selected at checkout.
- Lost or delayed: if the carrier confirms loss, offer a replacement or refund; delays alone do not qualify for an automatic refund.

# Allowed actions
- Help customers track shipments and interpret tracking updates.
- Open carrier investigations when deliveries appear delayed or missing.

# Disallowed actions
- Guarantee delivery dates or transit times.
- Cover duties or taxes unless explicitly authorized.

# Required wording
"Delivery times are estimates provided by carriers and may vary depending on location and customs processing."

# Escalation
Confirmed loss cases or systemic carrier delays.

# Examples
- A parcel is held by customs; advise the customer about estimated delays and duties.
- The carrier confirms a shipment is lost; offer a replacement or refund.

policies.title: Australian Consumer Law overlay for merchandise

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Australian Consumer Law (ACL) overrides store policy where applicable.

sections.heading: When it applies
sections.content: The shipping or billing address is in Australia.

sections.heading: Rules
sections.content:
- Goods must be of acceptable quality, fit for purpose, and as described.
- Remedies apply regardless of store warranty periods.
- Major fault: the customer chooses a refund or replacement.
- Minor fault: PBT may choose repair or replacement.
- Change of mind: ACL does not require refunds; the store policy may allow them at discretion.
- Overrides: base warranty limits do not restrict ACL rights.

sections.heading: Allowed actions
sections.content:
- Provide a refund or replacement for major faults at the customer's choice.
- Choose repair or replacement for minor faults.
- Apply the change-of-mind policy when it is offered as a courtesy.

sections.heading: Disallowed actions
sections.content:
- Deny ACL remedies due to store warranty periods or policy limits.
- State that change-of-mind refunds are required under ACL.

sections.heading: Required wording
sections.content: "Under Australian Consumer Law, you're entitled to a remedy if a product has a major fault."

sections.heading: Escalation
sections.content: Customer cites ACL, or the dispute involves high-value orders.

sections.heading: Examples
sections.content:
- A product fails due to a major defect; the customer chooses a refund under ACL.
- A customer requests a change-of-mind return; clarify that ACL does not require it and apply store policy if eligible.

policy_id: REF-MERCH-AU-001

  - "Support agents"

  - "australia"
  - "acl"
  - "consumer-law"
  - "returns"
  - "warranty"
---

# Summary
Australian Consumer Law (ACL) overrides store policy where applicable.

# When it applies
The shipping or billing address is in Australia.

# Rules
- Goods must be of acceptable quality, fit for purpose, and as described.
- Remedies apply regardless of store warranty periods.
- Major fault: the customer chooses a refund or replacement.
- Minor fault: PBT may choose repair or replacement.
- Change of mind: ACL does not require refunds; the store policy may allow them at discretion.
- Overrides: base warranty limits do not restrict ACL rights.

# Allowed actions
- Provide a refund or replacement for major faults at the customer's choice.
- Choose repair or replacement for minor faults.
- Apply the change-of-mind policy when it is offered as a courtesy.

# Disallowed actions
- Deny ACL remedies due to store warranty periods or policy limits.
- State that change-of-mind refunds are required under ACL.

# Required wording
"Under Australian Consumer Law, you're entitled to a remedy if a product has a major fault."

# Escalation
Customer cites ACL, or the dispute involves high-value orders.

# Examples
- A product fails due to a major defect; the customer chooses a refund under ACL.
- A customer requests a change-of-mind return; clarify that ACL does not require it and apply store policy if eligible.

policies.title: EU consumer law overlay for merchandise

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: EU statutory consumer rights override store return policies where applicable.

sections.heading: When it applies
sections.content: The shipping or billing address is in the European Union.

sections.heading: Rules
sections.content:
- Customers have a 14-day cooling-off period from receiving goods.
- Refunds include the product price and standard outbound shipping, and must be processed within 14 days of the return notice.
- Faulty goods carry a minimum 2-year guarantee.
- Overrides: store limits do not restrict statutory rights.

sections.heading: Allowed actions
sections.content:
- Approve cooling-off returns within 14 days of delivery.
- Issue refunds that include standard outbound shipping when applicable.
- Provide remedies for faulty goods within the minimum 2-year guarantee.

sections.heading: Disallowed actions
sections.content:
- Deny a valid cooling-off request made within 14 days.
- Exclude standard outbound shipping from a statutory refund.
- Reduce remedies below the 2-year guarantee for faulty goods.

sections.heading: Required wording
sections.content: "Under EU consumer law, you're entitled to a 14-day cooling-off period for online purchases."

sections.heading: Escalation
sections.content: Rights disputes or unclear eligibility.

sections.heading: Examples
sections.content:
- A customer notifies support within 14 days of delivery; process the cooling-off return.
- A defect appears after 18 months; the statutory guarantee still applies.

policies.raw_markdown:
---
policy_id: REF-MERCH-EU-001
title: "EU consumer law overlay for merchandise"
status: active
type: perpetual
domain: merchandise
visibility: public
effective_from: 2025-01-01
effective_to:
priority: 90
owner_team: "Policy Ops"
approvers:
  - "Head of Support"
jurisdiction:
  - "European Union"
applies_to:
  - "Support agents"
tags:
  - "eu"
  - "consumer-law"
  - "cooling-off"
  - "returns"
  - "warranty"
---

# Summary
EU statutory consumer rights override store return policies where applicable.

# When it applies
The shipping or billing address is in the European Union.

# Rules
- Customers have a 14-day cooling-off period from receiving goods.
- Refunds include the product price and standard outbound shipping, and must be processed within 14 days of the return notice.
- Faulty goods carry a minimum 2-year guarantee.
- Overrides: store limits do not restrict statutory rights.

# Allowed actions
- Approve cooling-off returns within 14 days of delivery.
- Issue refunds that include standard outbound shipping when applicable.
- Provide remedies for faulty goods within the minimum 2-year guarantee.

# Disallowed actions
- Deny a valid cooling-off request made within 14 days.
- Exclude standard outbound shipping from a statutory refund.
- Reduce remedies below the 2-year guarantee for faulty goods.

# Required wording
"Under EU consumer law, you're entitled to a 14-day cooling-off period for online purchases."

# Escalation
Rights disputes or unclear eligibility.

# Examples
- A customer notifies support within 14 days of delivery; process the cooling-off return.
- A defect appears after 18 months; the statutory guarantee still applies.
